source.description: Built-in image generation contact sheet reviewed before cropping; approved final panel is saved as the private draft finished artwork.
source.notes: This is a doodle/marker direction test, not a public daily lesson. Keep it out of the generated library until the marker style is approved.

process_strategy: Use one six-panel marker contact sheet that builds the flames from loose gesture paths to a bold black outline, nested inner flames, marker color fills, and final line/color strength.

final_elements.name: three tall flame tongues
final_elements.description: Three upward sweeping flame paths that define the main doodle rhythm.

final_elements.name: outer black marker silhouette
final_elements.description: One connected hot rod flame outline with rounded valleys and pointed tips.

final_elements.name: bold marker outline
final_elements.description: A heavier black felt-tip contour around the flame shape.

final_elements.name: inner flame tongues
final_elements.description: Two smaller nested flame shapes inside the main silhouette.

final_elements.name: orange marker fill
final_elements.description: Saturated orange marker fill across the outer flame area.

final_elements.name: yellow inner fill
final_elements.description: Yellow marker fill inside the nested flame shapes.

final_elements.name: red accent edges
final_elements.description: Small red marker accents along selected outer flame edges.

frames.visible_job: Sets a low baseline and three loose rising flame paths.
Connects the paths into one outer hot rod flame silhouette.
Strengthens the outer silhouette with a bold black marker contour.
Adds two nested inner flame tongues before any color appears.
Adds orange outer fill, yellow inner fill, and red edge accents with visible marker streaks.

final_step.visible_job: Only deepens the existing black outline and marker color fills without adding new flame tongues.
allowed_changes: paper and marker texture from the saved contact sheet

rejection_checks: Do not treat this as a realistic fire lesson; the target is a bold marker doodle.
No extra flame tongues, stickers, lettering, cars, smoke, or background props should appear in the final only.
The color step should look like marker fill, not colored pencil, watercolor, or airbrush.
Keep this draft unlinked and noindex until the doodle direction is approved.
